Class MeteredOutbox

java.lang.Object
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.micrometer.MeteredOutbox
All Implemented Interfaces:
Outbox
public final class MeteredOutbox extends Object implements Outbox
Decorator that adds Micrometer instrumentation to every publish and publishAll call without changing the contract of the underlying Outbox.

Meters emitted:

  • outbox.publish — a Timer recording publish latency (single-event path). Tags: aggregate_type, event_type, result (success or failure).
  • outbox.publish.bytes — a DistributionSummary recording per-event payload size in bytes. Tags: aggregate_type, event_type.
  • outbox.publish.batch — a Timer recording the latency of one publishAll call across the whole batch. Tag: result only.
  • outbox.publish.batch.size — a DistributionSummary recording the number of events per publishAll call. Untagged.

By design, aggregate_id and destination are never tagged, to keep metric cardinality bounded.

Cardinality cap

aggregate_type and event_type are emitted as tag values. If a caller derives either from user input the registry will allocate one time series per unique value, which is the canonical Micrometer cardinality bomb. To defend against this at runtime the decorator accepts an optional BiPredicate that decides whether a tag value should be kept; values that fail the predicate are replaced with a configurable fallback string (default "other"). The predicate is invoked once per meter registration with the tuple (tagName, value), where tagName is TAG_AGGREGATE_TYPE or TAG_EVENT_TYPE. The result tag is never sanitized — its domain is fixed by the library.

The default predicate ((n, v) -> true) preserves the pre-existing behavior, so existing callers see no change.

Instances are thread-safe: the MeterRegistry contract guarantees concurrent meter registration is safe, and the decorator holds no mutable state of its own.

Since:
0.1.0

    DEFAULT_TAG_FALLBACK
    Default replacement for tag values that fail the cardinality predicate.

    Description
    MeteredOutbox(Outbox delegate, MeterRegistry registry)
    Creates a new metering decorator around delegate with the default pass-through tag policy.

    Creates a new metering decorator around delegate with a custom tag-cardinality policy.

    • DEFAULT_TAG_FALLBACK

      public static final String DEFAULT_TAG_FALLBACK
      Default replacement for tag values that fail the cardinality predicate.
      Since:
      0.2.0

  • Constructor Details

    • MeteredOutbox

      public MeteredOutbox(Outbox delegate, MeterRegistry registry)
      Creates a new metering decorator around delegate with the default pass-through tag policy.
      Parameters:
      delegate - the underlying Outbox; never null.
      registry - the MeterRegistry that receives metrics; never null.
      Since:
      0.1.0

    • MeteredOutbox

      public MeteredOutbox(Outbox delegate, MeterRegistry registry, BiPredicate<String,String> tagValuePredicate, String tagFallback)
      Creates a new metering decorator around delegate with a custom tag-cardinality policy.
      Parameters:
      delegate - the underlying Outbox; never null.
      registry - the MeterRegistry that receives metrics; never null.
      tagValuePredicate - predicate that decides whether a (tagName, value) pair is allowed onto the registry. Values that return false are replaced with tagFallback. Never null.
      tagFallback - the replacement string used when tagValuePredicate rejects a value. Never null or blank.
      Since:
      0.2.0

  • Method Details

    • publish

      public void publish(OutboxEvent event)
      Description copied from interface: Outbox
      Persists the given event to the outbox table.

      The write is performed inside the caller's active transaction and is rolled back together with the caller's business writes if that transaction aborts.

      Specified by:
      publish in interface Outbox
      Parameters:
      event - the event to persist; never null.

    • publishAll

      public void publishAll(Iterable<OutboxEvent> events)
      Description copied from interface: Outbox
      Persists every event in events to the outbox table, atomically with respect to the caller's transaction.

      The default implementation simply iterates and calls Outbox.publish(OutboxEvent) per event. Implementations are encouraged to override this with a true batch write (e.g. JDBC addBatch / executeBatch) when the underlying store supports it. The default exists for source compatibility — every Outbox produced before this method was added still satisfies the contract.

      If any individual write fails, the surrounding transaction will not be committed by this library; the failure surfaces as an OutboxException subtype on the offending event.

      Specified by:
      publishAll in interface Outbox
      Parameters:
      events - the events to persist; never null, individual elements must not be null.
